out_of_scope_observations.reason: Tracked test files assert the OLD error token 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' but the changed hooks now emit 'bulk-commit-sentinel-write'. These tests will fail at runtime due to the token rename.
out_of_scope_observations.recommendation: QA regression gate should catch this. Test files need their STABLE_STDERR_TOKEN / expected_stderr_token / assertion strings updated from 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' to 'bulk-commit-sentinel-write'.

detect_orphan_agents.note: 1 orphan agent (product-owner.md) is pre-existing, not introduced by this diff

detect_orphan_scripts.note: All 17 orphan scripts are pre-existing, not introduced by this diff

detect_duplicate_content.note: All duplicate groups are pre-existing, not introduced by this diff

summary.notes: All three changed files are internally consistent. The auth-flag concept (/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag) was cleanly removed from all three hooks. The error token was consistently renamed from 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' to 'bulk-commit-sentinel-write' across all changed files. No dangling references remain in the changed files. One out-of-scope QA regression risk noted: 29 tracked test files reference the old error token and will need updating (QA regression-gate scope, not cleanliness scope per --changed-files contract).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 issues, all with PROPOSED_FIX. All 4 applied: (1) the test-token mismatch finding was moved from blocking cleanliness findings to non-blocking out_of_scope_observations; (2) reclassified from obsolete_functionality to qa_regression_risk; (3) severity downgraded from major to info with blocking:false; (4) introduced_in_diff changed from true to false with caused_by_diff:true and scope_status:out_of_scope.

findings.issue: Scope contract violation: obsolete_functionality finding targets test files outside --changed-files set. Indirect regression breakage is QA scope, not cleanliness scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from cleanliness findings or move to non-blocking out_of_scope_observations section.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct. The --changed-files scope contract explicitly states 'indirect breakage in untouched files is QA regression-gate scope, not inspector cleanliness scope.' Moved to out_of_scope_observations.

findings.issue: Misclassified category: stale test expectations are qa_regression_risk, not obsolete_functionality.
findings.rationale: Agreed. The test token mismatch is a regression risk, not a cleanliness/obsolete-functionality issue within the changed files.

findings.issue: Misclassified severity: major is inappropriate for an out-of-scope finding.
findings.proposed_fix: Downgrade to severity: info, blocking: false.
findings.rationale: Agreed. Out-of-scope observations should not carry blocking severity.

findings.issue: introduced_in_diff: true is misleading when affected files are not in --changed-files.
findings.proposed_fix: Use introduced_in_diff: false with caused_by_diff: true and scope_status: out_of_scope.
findings.rationale: Agreed. The tag introduced_in_diff should only apply to files within the --changed-files scope. Using caused_by_diff: true captures the causal relationship without triggering AC-2.6(b) NEW-positive marker blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex findings applied. Draft restructured: (1) test-token finding removed from findings.obsolete_functionality and placed in out_of_scope_observations; (2) category changed to qa_regression_risk; (3) severity changed to info, blocking: false; (4) introduced_in_diff: false, caused_by_diff: true, scope_status: out_of_scope.
